steps.title: Expand and identify when a linear equation has no solution
steps.content:
Expanding the left side:
$$5kx - 5n = -\frac{65}{14}x - \frac{85}{18}$$
A linear equation has no solution when the coefficients of $x$ are equal but the constants differ. This means:
$$5k = -\frac{65}{14}$$

steps.title: Solve for k
steps.content:
$$k = -\frac{65}{70} = -\frac{13}{14}$$
Check: With this value, the constants are $-5n$ and $-\frac{85}{18}$. Since $n > 1$ implies $5n > 5 > \frac{85}{18}$, we have $-5n \neq -\frac{85}{18}$, confirming no solution exists.
